item.title: Best laptops in 2025
item.itunes:title: Best laptops in 2025
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Buying a great new laptop can be an exhilarating feeling, but it can also be a daunting task. Whether you're shopping for a new work partner, gaming laptop, or something in between, there's a never-ending sea of options to browse through, so it can be challenging. We've reviewed a lot of laptops here at XDA, and to help you out, we've compiled the best ones into this list.

There are many factors to consider, including screen size, form, performance, and overall design. It's not the same for everyone, either. The specs that make a great laptop for work don't make a great one for gaming, and vice-versa. And while a gaming laptop can handle other kinds of tasks just fine, it will be very expensive and heavy. The choices we've rounded up below cover a good range of use cases, and we're confident that you'll find something that's perfectly suited to your needs.

  • HP OmniBook Ultra Flip 14
    Best overall
    $1020 at HP
  • Apple MacBook Air (M3, 2024)
    Runner-up
    $899 at Best Buy (13 inches)
  • Dell XPS 13 (2024, Intel Lunar Lake)
    Best clamshell
    $1200 at Dell (Qualcomm)
  • Surface Laptop 7
    Also great
    $797 at Amazon (13 inches)
  • Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6
    Best business
    $1271 at Lenovo
  • Surface Pro 11
    Best Windows tablet
    $900 at Amazon
  • Acer Predator Helios 16 (2024)
    Best gaming laptop
    $1900 at Acer
  • MacBook Pro (M3, 2023)
    Best for creators
    $1699 at Best Buy (14-inch, M3 Pro/Max)
  • Dell XPS 16
    Best 16-inch
    $1700 at Dell
  • Image credit: Dell
    Dell Inspiron 14 (2024, Qualcomm)
    Best budget laptop
    $750 at Dell

Our favorite laptops in 2024

Your changes have been saved

Email is sent

Email has already been sent

Best overall
HP OmniBook Ultra Flip 14

What else could you ask for?

9.5 / 10
$1020 $1449 Save $429

Powered by Intel Lunar Lake processors, HP's latest convertible is better than ever. It offers excellent performance for both CPU and GPU while being far more efficient than previous generations, all combined with a versatile and unique design, a stunning display, and one of the best webcam in any laptop.

Pros
  • Intel Lunar Lake processors deliver top-tier performance and efficiency
  • Beautiful OLED display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Great keyboard an touchpad
  • 9MP webcam is one of the best you can find on a laptop
Cons
  • Port layout is a bit strange
  • Expensive
$1020 at HP $1600 at Best Buy

HP knows how to make a mean laptop when it wants to, and the OmniBook Ultra Flip 14 immediately shot to the top of our recommendations once we reviewed it. This is a nearly perfect laptop that's only held back by being a bit pricy, but you definitely get your money's worth here.

Inside the OmniBook Ultra Flip 14 are Intel's Lunar Lake processors, which are better than the previous generation in almost every way. Graphics performance is leagues better and makes gaming possible on such a thin and light device, but it's also incredibly efficient compared to previous models, making battery life some of the best you can get on any Windows laptop. And with up to 32GB of RAM and 2TB of storage, you can get all the performance you could want here.

 Related
HP OmniBook Ultra Flip review: Literally everyone should buy this

It's true to the Spectre x360 brand

9

Then there's the fact that you get an absolutely stunning display. This 14-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio uses OLED technology, meaning blacks are perfectly black regardless of how bright your screen is. Pair that with the sharp 2.8K resolution and the buttery smooth 120Hz refresh rate, and you have a visual experience that's an absolute treat for the eyes. On top of that, you have a 9MP webcam, the best on any laptop out there.

Round things out with an excellent keyboard and touchpad and one of the more unique laptop designs you can get, and you have a nearly perfect laptop. We only wish there was a Thunderbolt port on each side, but with two Thunderbolt ports in total, plus a standard USB-C port and a headphone jack, it's hard to complain too much. This is simply a phenomenal laptop, and if you're buying any laptop today, it should be this one.

Your changes have been saved

Email is sent

Email has already been sent

Runner-up
Apple MacBook Air (M3, 2024)

A lightweight alternative with great battery life

9 / 10
$899 $1099 Save $200

Apple keeps improving its MacBooks with better performance and efficiency, and the M3 MacBook Air is an absolute delight that offers a lot of power while still having unrivaled battery life so you can work all day without a charger. You can choose between 13-inch and 15-inch models, too.

Pros
  • Apple M3 processor delivers excellent performance and battery life
  • Sharp display with P3 Wide Color in both 13- and 15-inch options
  • Very thin and light
Cons
  • Two external displays are only supported if the laptop is closed
  • macOS may not be for everyone
$899 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1099 at Best Buy (15 inches) $899 at Amazon (13 inches) $1099 at Amazon (15 inches)

Not everyone is a Windows fan, and the latest MacBook Air is one of the best laptops if you prefer macOS, or even if you just want to try something new. Thanks to the super-efficient Apple Silicon, the latest iteration of the MacBook Air is "one of the best mainstream laptops in the world", as our reviewer Ben Sin put it.

2:15
Related
MacBook Air (M3, 15-inch, 2024) review: The Goldilocks laptop size

The 15-inch MacBook Air offers an immersive large screen experience while keeping a slim and light(er) body than the MacBook Pro

This latest edition comes with with Apple M3 processor, which is the first of the Apple Silicon line to use a 3nm manufacturing process to be even more efficient. It still has the same number of CPU cores, but it's a good bit faster now, and on the GPU side, there are completely new cores that take performance to the next level. If you're editing video, this machine can handle that kind of workload comfortably.

The MacBook Air is available in two sizes: 13.6 inches and 15.3 inches. Both have great panels with up to 500 nits of brightness and support for P3 Wide Color and True Tone, and they're nearly identical otherwise. The smaller model has a resolution of 2560x1664, while the larger model goes up to 2880x1864 resolution. They share the same pixels-per-inch count making them equally as sharp. One thing you may not like is the notch at the top of the screen that houses the clear 1080p webcam.

The M3 MacBook Air retains the same design as its predecessor, featuring a flat unibody construction that's very thin and light, yet still feels premium. The chassis is entirely made of aluminum, and it comes in four color options, including Silver, Space Grey, Midnight, and Starlight. There aren't a ton of ports in this design, which includes two Thunderbolt / USB4 ports, a headphone jack with support for high-impedance headphones, and a MagSafe connector for charging. Unlike previous models, you can use two external displays, but you'll have to close the lid on the laptop to turn off the built-in screen.

Your changes have been saved

Email is sent

Email has already been sent

Best clamshell
Dell XPS 13 (2024)

Performance, efficiency, and a beautiful design

9.5 / 10

Packed with Intel's new Core Ultra Series 2 or QUalcomm Snapdragon X Elite processors, the Dell XPS 13 is better than ever. With a sleek design, excellent display options, and an unrivaled mix of performance and battery life, this is simply one of the best laptops on the market.

Pros
  • Both Intel and Qualcomm versions deliver top-tier performance and efficiency
  • Sleek, futurustic design
  • Excellent display options
Cons
  • Only two USB-C ports and nothing else
$1400 at Best Buy $1250 at Dell $1200 at Dell (Qualcomm)

The Dell XPS 13 is no stranger to the spotlight, being one of the best laptops on the market each and every year. And now, it's better than ever thanks to the latest processors from both Intel and Qualcomm.

Qualcomm really changed the game with its Snapdragon X Elite processor, but now Intel's Lunar Lake processors have caught up. No matter which version you get, this laptop offers fantastic performance and efficiency, so you can enjoy a great experience even while on battery power and without sacrificing battery life. Battery life is especially great on the Qualcomm model, but the Intel version is more widely compatible with apps and games, so it's a bit easier to recommend to anyone. You can also configure this laptop with up to 64GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

 Related
Dell XPS 13 (2024, Lunar Lake) review: Intel is so back

Battery life exceeds expectations

10

On top of the excellent processor options, the Dell XPS 13 Plus also has some great display options. The base model is a relatively basic, but still quite good Full HD+ 120Hz panel, but you can upgrade to a sharper Quad HD+ display or, if you want the best of the best, opt for the stunning 2.8K OLED display, which also uses the fancy Tandem OLED technology you've probably heard Apple talk about for its latest iPad Pro. No matter your choice of screen, though, you'll get a great experience.

 Related
Dell XPS 13 9345 review: A winner no matter the chipset

The Dell XPS 13 now comes with a Qualcomm chipset and is just as winsome as its Intel brethren.

All of this comes packaged in a beautifully slender design, with a zero-lattice edge-edge keyboard and an invisible touchpad that make this laptop feel like it comes from the future, even if this design is two years old at this point. There's still nothing like it. You only get two USB-C ports, which is a bit of a bummer, but aside from that, this is a nearly perfect laptop.

Your changes have been saved

Email is sent

Email has already been sent

Also great
Surface Laptop 7

Sleek, stylish, and powerful

9 / 10
$797 $1000 Save $203

The Surface Laptop 7 is one of the first laptops powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon X series processors, and it's also the first big shakeup of the Surface Laptop line. It has a much more modern design with slimmer bezels and a better display that makes this an namazing laptop.

Pros
  • Qualcomm Snapdragon X Plus and X Elite processors deliver unrivalled performance and eficicnecy
  • Updated design finally feels modern and comes in fun colors
  • Significantly better display than previous generations
Cons
  • Some games still won't work
  • No OLED display option
$797 at Amazon (13 inches) $1197 at Amazon (15 inches) $1000 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1300 at Best Buy (15 inches)

Microsoft's Surface Laptop 7 is the best laptop the company has ever made, and it's a very necessary update to one of its most popular products.

Inside the Surface Laptop 7 is a Qualcomm Snapdragon X Plus or X Elite chipset, and this is a big deal. This is an Arm-based processor with a fully custom design that delivers performance we;ve never seen from WIjdows on Arm in the past. In a lot of cases, it's faster than any current Intel processor, and on top of that, it's far more power efficient. You can get some insane battery life numbers from this laptop, withj our reviewer Rich Woods getting over 15 hours of battery life at one point. You also get up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

 Related
Surface Laptop 7 review: You don't have to wait for Windows on Arm to get good anymore

It does most things right

To go along with the brand-new processor, Microsoft also gave the Surface Laptop 7 a completely new design. Both models are now smaller, and in the case of the 13-inch version, you even get a slightly larger 13.8-inch panel (while still being smaller than its predecessor). This change was long overdue, and to make things better, the screen has also been improved with a brighter panel and better color reproduction.

For ports, the Surface Laptop 7 includes two high-speed USB4 connections, a USB Type-A port, a custom Surface Connect port, and a microSD card reader, plus a headphone jack. That's also an upgrade from previous models, and even without proper Thunderbolt, USB4 gives us capabilities that are almost identical (though external GPUs won't work here).

Your changes have been saved

Email is sent

Email has already been sent

Best business
Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6

Durable and reliable

9 / 10
$1622 $1699 Save $77

Powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon X Eolite, the Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6 delivers solid performance and excellent battery life in a chassis designed for professionals. It has the same excellent keyboard Lenovo is known for, and even the speakers are quite good.

Pros
  • Qualcomm Snapdragon X Elite chip is fast and super efficient
  • Excellent keyboard and a classic design
  • Powerful speakers
Cons
  • Display in the base model isn't that bright
  • It has a lower tier of the Snapdragon X ELite compared to some other models
$1271 at Lenovo $1393 at Amazon $1622 at CDW

Normally, you might expect to see a Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon in this spot, but the company decided to go with the T14s to debut the new QUalcomm Snapdragon X Elite chip, and frankly,. that alone makes this the laptop to choose if you're looking for a business laptop.

The Snapdragon X Elite chip in the Thinkpad T14s Gen 6 isn't the most high-end configuration available, but make no mistake, this is an excellent chip all the same, and it delivers more performance than its Intel rivals while also being much more efficient, resulting in an experience you can't really get on Intel-based laptops. It lasts over 14 hours on battery, according to our reviewer, who also said the laptop is "perfect for mobile professionals". Aside from the processor, you can also get up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD.

 Related
Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6 (14" Snapdragon) review: A near-perfect business laptop

The Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6 has it all - great performance, long battery life, a comfortable keyboard, and potent speakers in a ultra-light chassis

The model we reviewed came with the base display, which is a FUll HD+ IPS panel, and it could be a bit brighter and vibrant. But if you want that, you can opt for the stunning 2.8K OLED panel, which also comes with a smooth 120Hz refresh rate. If you want the best experience, this is absolutely the way to go, but it's not necessary if you're just using the laptop for work.

The design is as classic ThinkPad as it gets, with the fully black chassis accented by some red highlights, including the iconic TrackPoint and the duplicate mouse buttons above the touchpad. Of course, it has military-grade durability certification, too, so this is a laptop that can last you a long time. And as more developers embrace Windows on Arm, it will probably be getting better over time, too.

Your changes have been saved

Email is sent

Email has already been sent

Best Windows tablet
Surface Pro 11

Fast, efficient, and super portable

$900 $1000 Save $100

The Surface Pro 11 is the best Windows tablet on the market right now. Powered by a Snapdragon X Elite, it's both fast and efficient with great battery life, and it has a better display than ever, including a new OLED option. It's not the best Windows tablet now, it's the best Surface Pro ever.

Pros
  • Snapdragon X Elite makes it faster than ever while having great battery life
  • It's the best Surface Pro display ever
  • The new Flex Keyboard is a big upgrade
Cons
  • Windows still isn't the best operating system for tablets
$1000 at Best Buy $900 at Amazon

As far as Windows tablets are concerned, no brand is as well-known as the Surface Pro, and for good reason. They've always been some of the best tablets you can buy, and with the Surface Pro 111, that's more true than ever before.

Starting on the inside, the Surface Pro 11 comes with the brand-new Snapdragon X series chips, with options for the Snapdragon X Plus or X Elite. No matter what you choose, you're getting a very powerful machine that can outclass a typical Intel PC in a lot of use cases. But more importantly, it's also much more efficient, so battery life is better than it's ever been on a Surface Pro. As our Editor-in-Chief Rich Woods put it in his review, "it's truly exciting to see a Windows tablet with...tablet battery life".

 Related
Surface Pro 11 review: Microsoft almost gets it right

A great laptop with a bad tablet OS

But it doesn't stop there. This is also the best display Microsoft has ever used on a Surface Pro. Previous displays were good, but not super vibrant and very reflective. Now, Microsoft made it less glossy and you have an OLED option for the first time, which looks great. It's also a very sharp screen with a 120Hz refresh rate, so it's great all around.

If you want to turn the Surface Pro 11 into a proper laptop, Microsoft has also introduced the Flex Keyboard. It's sold separately and pretty expensive, at $400, but it now gives you the ability to use it detached from the tablet, so you have a lot more flexibility. You can use it as a screen cover, but you can also push the screen back if you want to get some distance from your display.

All in all, the Surface Pro 11 is a fantastic device that's easy to recommend to anyone wanting a Windows-powered tablet. We suspect it will remain that way for a long while to come.

Your changes have been saved

Email is sent

Email has already been sent

Best gaming laptop
Acer Predator Helios 16 (2024)

So much power

9 / 10
$2050 $2300 Save $250

The Acer Predator Helios is a very powerful gaming laptop with top-notch processors and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4080 GPU that can can handle any game without issues. It has lso an excellent display with a 250Hz refresh rate, perfect for the most demanding gamers.

Pros
  • Incredible performance from both the CPU and GPU
  • Excellent display with a super smooth 250Hz refresh rate
  • Lovely design with plenty of RGB
Cons
  • Fans get very loud
  • Battery life is bad
$1900 at Acer $2050 at Micro Center

Gaming laptops need a lot more power than your average laptop, and the Acer Predator Helios 16 is more than happy to deliver on that front. It has all the performance you could want and an excellent display to boot, plus it looks cool.

Starting with performance, we have a laptop with 14th-gen Intel Core HX-series processors, paired with up to an Nvidia geForce RTX 4080 Laptop GPU, which can use the full 175W of power it's designed for. You won't be hungry for power, and any and all games should run easily on this laptop without much sacrifice. In fact, our reviewer called it "one of the most powerful and one of the best laptops of the year", which is very high praise. You do get bad battery life as a result, but that's expected for a gaming laptop.

If that's not enough to convince you, consider this: the Acer Predator comes with a 16-inch QUad HD+ display with a 250Hz refresh rate. That high resolution and refresh rate are frankly more than the laptop's power can deliver in most games, but if you have an esports title you want to play as smoothly as possible, this display delivers. And that's not even mentioning the excellent color reproduction, since the display covers 99% of DCI-P3 based on our measurements.

A powerful laptop like this has to be big and bulky, so portability is a notable sacrifice, but you do get a striking design with a ton of RGB lighting making sure your laptop is going to turn heads if you use it in public.You also get plenty of ports, so there's very little to complain about here.

Your changes have been saved

Email is sent

Email has already been sent

Best for creators
MacBook Pro (M3, 2023)

So much performance with great battery life

$1699 $1999 Save $300

Despite already having the best creator laptop on the market, Apple updated the MacBook pro for a second time in 2023 with the M3 family of processors, making the CPU and GPU even more powerful and efficient. The latest MacBook Pro is an absolutely fantastic machine for creative workloads, and it can last much longer on battery than any comparable Windows laptop.

Pros
  • Apple M3 Pro and M3 Max processors are ideal for all kinds of content creation
  • Battery life is fantastic thanks to the new 3nm Arm processors
  • Stunning mini-LED display with up to 1600 nits of brightness
  • Six-speaker system makes for an excellent media experience
Cons
  • Some might not like the notch
  • Expensive, especially with storage and RAM upgrades
  • The base M3 14-inch model isn't great for demanding workloads
$1699 at Best Buy (14-inch, M3 Pro/Max) $2399 at Best Buy (16-inch, M3 Pro/Max)

Apple was already way ahead of the pack when it comes to creator laptops, but that didn't stop the company from introducing even more powerful and efficient MacBook Pro models in late 2023 despite the previous models being less than a year old. XDA Senior Editor Ben Sin reviewed the MacBook Pro 16 (M3 Max) and mentioned that those with an M2 model likely won't see a need to upgrade. For everyone else, however, it's the go-to model.

 Related
MacBook Pro 16 (M3 Max) review: Apple’s just flexing

The 10-month old M2 Max MacBook Pro is still more powerful than 99% of laptops out there, then Apple released an upgrade with more graphical prowess

The latest MacBook Pro models feature the M3 family of processors, and creators will want to focus on the M3 Pro and M3 Max versions. These processors are based on a new 3nm process, making them even more efficient than before, delivering unparalleled battery life while offering excellent performance for demanding workloads like video editing and 3D rendering. Of course, they're also faster than ever, particularly the M3 Max model, featuring a faster CPU and a totally new GPU with ray-tracing support. You also get more RAM than ever, with up to 192GB of unified memory, along with up to 8TB of storage.

In terms of the display, the MacBook Pro comes with a 14.2-inch or 16.2-inch mini-LED panel, which are both fantastic. The screen has hundreds of individual dimming zones, delivering high contrast and brightness up to a whopping 1,600 nits, making for a terrific viewing experience. For this generation, the SDR brightness has also been increased to 600 nits, so visibility should be even better outdoors. The resolution varies depending on the size, but both are equally sharp, and to go along with that, you get a six-speaker system, resulting in one of the best media experiences on any laptop. The webcam is also a great 1080p sensor, though there's no facial recognition support. You can use Touch ID, though.

The design of the MacBook Pro is fairly standard, being an all-metal laptop with a clamshell design and starting at 3.5 pounds (for the 14-inch M3 Pro model), which is reasonable considering how powerful it is. The M3 Pro and M3 Max variants also come in a new Space Black colorway, in addition to the classic Silver. As for ports, you get three Thunderbolt 4 connections, along with HDMI 2.1, a headphone jack with support for high-impedance headphones, an SD card reader, and MagSafe 3 for charging.

Dell XPS 16

Dell's new XPS size looks great

8 / 10

Dell's XPS 16 is the new best option for power users and pros who desire extra performance and a larger display. It's taking over for the XPS 17, and it's been redesigned to match the futuristic XPS 13 Plus look that was introduced a couple of years ago.

Pros
  • Beautiful displays with plenty of room to work
  • Lots of performance thanks to Intel Core Ultra CPUs and discrete Nvidia GPUs

$1700 at Dell $2200 at Best Buy

Part of Dell's 2024 overhaul of the XPS brand is a new 16-inch form factor. It's replacing the XPS 17 as the largest XPS laptop on the market, and it should remain the right choice for power users and pros who need extra screen real estate and power. It features the same seamless glass touchpad with haptics, capacitive touch function row, and near-gapless keyboard that we first saw on the XPS 13 Plus.

Dell offers up to an Intel Core Ultra 9 185H CPU with 16 cores, up to 64GB of LPDDR5x-7467MHz RAM, and 4TB of M.2 PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSD storage. Integrated Arc graphics come with all models, but you can also spec up to either a Nvidia RTX 4050, RTX 4060, or RTX 4070 Laptop GPU.

Dell XPS 16 (2024) review: The MacBook Pro of Windows

The Dell XPS 16 is like nothing you've seen before.

The 16.3-inch display comes in two flavors, with an FHD+ resolution being the cheapest option. It has about 500 nits brightness, 100% sRGB color, maximum 120Hz refresh rate, Dolby Vision, and an anti-glare finish. You can also upgrade to a touchscreen with UHD+ resolution. Best budget laptop
Dell Inspiron 14 (2024, Qualcomm)

Versatile and capable at a reasonable price

The Dell Inspiron 14 may be affordable, but it still offers a fantastic experience as a budget laptop. The Snapdragon X Plus chip is surprisingly fast and provides terrific battery life, all while running quietly and cool. It's still a cheap laptop with some compromises, but it's an easy recommendation at this price.

Pros
  • Surprisingly great performance, even with the lowest tier processor
  • Fantastic battery life
  • Solid build quality
Cons
  • Color reproduction isn't very vibrant
  • Windows on Arm may still struggle with app compatibility at times
$800 at Best Buy $750 at Dell

Buying a cheap laptop comes with some necessary sacrifices, but thanks to the latest Dell Inspiron 14, those sacrifices don't have to be in performance nor battery life.

It's all thanks to Qualcomm's new Snapdragon processors, which have truly shaken things up for the industry. The base model here starts with a Snapdragon X Plus 8-core, the lowest tier available, but its CPU performance is on par with some of the best CPUs from Intel or AMD. Seriously, no processor from those brands can even compare in this price range. And this is all while being extremely efficient, meaning battery life is far better on this chip, too. If you have the budget, you can also upgrade to the Snapdragon X Plus 10-core for even better performance.

 Related
Dell Inspiron 14 5441 (Qualcomm) review: The entry-level Windows on Arm laptop to buy

There were a few compromises made to hit this appealing price, however.

2

The sacrifices start with the display, which isn't particularly great here. It is a Full HD+ 16:10 panel, but in this price range, you're sacrificing viewing angles and color reproduction, so things won't look as pretty as you'd like them to. However, that will be true for almost any laptop at this price unless you're making big compromises in performance or functionality.

The design overall is also not the most premium, since this is a fairly thick laptop, but again, that's par for the course in this price. And considering there's actually a good bit of metal in this chassis, it's actually more premium than many of its peers. It has a solid keyboard, too, and the port selection is respectable, with two USB-C ports, one USB Type-A, and a headphone jack.

For this price, it's nearly impossible to recommend anything else other than the Dell Inspiron 14.

Choosing the best laptop for you

These are all excellent laptops to choose from, and it's really just a matter of choosing what suits your specific needs and budget the best. Our top pick has to go to the HP OmniBook Ultra Flip because it simply does nearly everything right. It has the top-tier performance and efficiency of Intel's Lunar Lake processors, a versatile convertible design, a stunning OLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate, and an excellent keyboard and trouchpad. Unless you want a high-end gaming rig, it's very hard to go wrong here.

But any other option on this list is fantastic. Myself, I love a convertible or 2-in-1 PC, so something like the Surface Pro 11 would also be a strong contender. But if you're looking to save some money, then the Dell Inspiron 14 is probably the one for you.

item.description:
Buying a great new laptop can be an exhilarating feeling, but it can also be a daunting task. Whether you're shopping for a new work partner, gaming laptop, or something in between, there's a never-ending sea of options to browse through, so it can be challenging. We've reviewed a lot of laptops here at XDA, and to help you out, we've compiled the best ones into this list.

There are many factors to consider, including screen size, form, performance, and overall design. It's not the same for everyone, either. The specs that make a great laptop for work don't make a great one for gaming, and vice-versa. And while a gaming laptop can handle other kinds of tasks just fine, it will be very expensive and heavy. The choices we've rounded up below cover a good range of use cases, and we're confident that you'll find something that's perfectly suited to your needs.

  • HP OmniBook Ultra Flip 14
    Best overall
    $1020 at HP
  • Apple MacBook Air (M3, 2024)
    Runner-up
    $899 at Best Buy (13 inches)
  • Dell XPS 13 (2024, Intel Lunar Lake)
    Best clamshell
    $1200 at Dell (Qualcomm)
  • Surface Laptop 7
    Also great
    $797 at Amazon (13 inches)
  • Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6
    Best business
    $1271 at Lenovo
  • Surface Pro 11
    Best Windows tablet
    $900 at Amazon
  • Acer Predator Helios 16 (2024)
    Best gaming laptop
    $1900 at Acer
  • MacBook Pro (M3, 2023)
    Best for creators
    $1699 at Best Buy (14-inch, M3 Pro/Max)

    Best 16-inch

Best overall
HP OmniBook Ultra Flip 14

What else could you ask for?

9.5 / 10
$1020 $1449 Save $429

Powered by Intel Lunar Lake processors, HP's latest convertible is better than ever. It offers excellent performance for both CPU and GPU while being far more efficient than previous generations, all combined with a versatile and unique design, a stunning display, and one of the best webcam in any laptop.

Pros
  • Intel Lunar Lake processors deliver top-tier performance and efficiency
  • Beautiful OLED display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Great keyboard an touchpad
  • 9MP webcam is one of the best you can find on a laptop
Cons
  • Port layout is a bit strange
  • Expensive
$1020 at HP $1600 at Best Buy

HP knows how to make a mean laptop when it wants to, and the OmniBook Ultra Flip 14 immediately shot to the top of our recommendations once we reviewed it. This is a nearly perfect laptop that's only held back by being a bit pricy, but you definitely get your money's worth here.

Inside the OmniBook Ultra Flip 14 are Intel's Lunar Lake processors, which are better than the previous generation in almost every way. Graphics performance is leagues better and makes gaming possible on such a thin and light device, but it's also incredibly efficient compared to previous models, making battery life some of the best you can get on any Windows laptop. And with up to 32GB of RAM and 2TB of storage, you can get all the performance you could want here.

 Related
HP OmniBook Ultra Flip review: Literally everyone should buy this

It's true to the Spectre x360 brand

9

Then there's the fact that you get an absolutely stunning display. This 14-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio uses OLED technology, meaning blacks are perfectly black regardless of how bright your screen is. Pair that with the sharp 2.8K resolution and the buttery smooth 120Hz refresh rate, and you have a visual experience that's an absolute treat for the eyes. On top of that, you have a 9MP webcam, the best on any laptop out there.

Round things out with an excellent keyboard and touchpad and one of the more unique laptop designs you can get, and you have a nearly perfect laptop. We only wish there was a Thunderbolt port on each side, but with two Thunderbolt ports in total, plus a standard USB-C port and a headphone jack, it's hard to complain too much. This is simply a phenomenal laptop, and if you're buying any laptop today, it should be this one.

Runner-up
Apple MacBook Air (M3, 2024)

A lightweight alternative with great battery life

9 / 10
$899 $1099 Save $200

Apple keeps improving its MacBooks with better performance and efficiency, and the M3 MacBook Air is an absolute delight that offers a lot of power while still having unrivaled battery life so you can work all day without a charger. You can choose between 13-inch and 15-inch models, too.

Pros
  • Apple M3 processor delivers excellent performance and battery life
  • Sharp display with P3 Wide Color in both 13- and 15-inch options
  • Very thin and light
Cons
  • Two external displays are only supported if the laptop is closed
  • macOS may not be for everyone
$899 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1099 at Best Buy (15 inches) $899 at Amazon (13 inches) $1099 at Amazon (15 inches)

Not everyone is a Windows fan, and the latest MacBook Air is one of the best laptops if you prefer macOS, or even if you just want to try something new. Thanks to the super-efficient Apple Silicon, the latest iteration of the MacBook Air is "one of the best mainstream laptops in the world", as our reviewer Ben Sin put it.

2:15
Related
MacBook Air (M3, 15-inch, 2024) review: The Goldilocks laptop size

The 15-inch MacBook Air offers an immersive large screen experience while keeping a slim and light(er) body than the MacBook Pro

This latest edition comes with with Apple M3 processor, which is the first of the Apple Silicon line to use a 3nm manufacturing process to be even more efficient. It still has the same number of CPU cores, but it's a good bit faster now, and on the GPU side, there are completely new cores that take performance to the next level. If you're editing video, this machine can handle that kind of workload comfortably.

The MacBook Air is available in two sizes: 13.6 inches and 15.3 inches. Both have great panels with up to 500 nits of brightness and support for P3 Wide Color and True Tone, and they're nearly identical otherwise. The smaller model has a resolution of 2560x1664, while the larger model goes up to 2880x1864 resolution. They share the same pixels-per-inch count making them equally as sharp. One thing you may not like is the notch at the top of the screen that houses the clear 1080p webcam.

The M3 MacBook Air retains the same design as its predecessor, featuring a flat unibody construction that's very thin and light, yet still feels premium. The chassis is entirely made of aluminum, and it comes in four color options, including Silver, Space Grey, Midnight, and Starlight. There aren't a ton of ports in this design, which includes two Thunderbolt / USB4 ports, a headphone jack with support for high-impedance headphones, and a MagSafe connector for charging. Unlike previous models, you can use two external displays, but you'll have to close the lid on the laptop to turn off the built-in screen.

Best clamshell
Dell XPS 13 (2024)

Performance, efficiency, and a beautiful design

9.5 / 10

Packed with Intel's new Core Ultra Series 2 or QUalcomm Snapdragon X Elite processors, the Dell XPS 13 is better than ever. With a sleek design, excellent display options, and an unrivaled mix of performance and battery life, this is simply one of the best laptops on the market.

Pros
  • Both Intel and Qualcomm versions deliver top-tier performance and efficiency
  • Sleek, futurustic design
  • Excellent display options
Cons
  • Only two USB-C ports and nothing else
$1400 at Best Buy $1250 at Dell $1200 at Dell (Qualcomm)

The Dell XPS 13 is no stranger to the spotlight, being one of the best laptops on the market each and every year. And now, it's better than ever thanks to the latest processors from both Intel and Qualcomm.

Qualcomm really changed the game with its Snapdragon X Elite processor, but now Intel's Lunar Lake processors have caught up. No matter which version you get, this laptop offers fantastic performance and efficiency, so you can enjoy a great experience even while on battery power and without sacrificing battery life. Battery life is especially great on the Qualcomm model, but the Intel version is more widely compatible with apps and games, so it's a bit easier to recommend to anyone. You can also configure this laptop with up to 64GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

 Related
Dell XPS 13 (2024, Lunar Lake) review: Intel is so back

Battery life exceeds expectations

10

On top of the excellent processor options, the Dell XPS 13 Plus also has some great display options. The base model is a relatively basic, but still quite good Full HD+ 120Hz panel, but you can upgrade to a sharper Quad HD+ display or, if you want the best of the best, opt for the stunning 2.8K OLED display, which also uses the fancy Tandem OLED technology you've probably heard Apple talk about for its latest iPad Pro. No matter your choice of screen, though, you'll get a great experience.

 Related
Dell XPS 13 9345 review: A winner no matter the chipset

The Dell XPS 13 now comes with a Qualcomm chipset and is just as winsome as its Intel brethren.

All of this comes packaged in a beautifully slender design, with a zero-lattice edge-edge keyboard and an invisible touchpad that make this laptop feel like it comes from the future, even if this design is two years old at this point. There's still nothing like it. You only get two USB-C ports, which is a bit of a bummer, but aside from that, this is a nearly perfect laptop.

Surface Laptop 7

Sleek, stylish, and powerful

9 / 10
$797 $1000 Save $203

The Surface Laptop 7 is one of the first laptops powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon X series processors, and it's also the first big shakeup of the Surface Laptop line. It has a much more modern design with slimmer bezels and a better display that makes this an namazing laptop.

Pros
  • Qualcomm Snapdragon X Plus and X Elite processors deliver unrivalled performance and eficicnecy
  • Updated design finally feels modern and comes in fun colors
  • Significantly better display than previous generations
Cons
  • Some games still won't work
  • No OLED display option
$797 at Amazon (13 inches) $1197 at Amazon (15 inches) $1000 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1300 at Best Buy (15 inches)

Microsoft's Surface Laptop 7 is the best laptop the company has ever made, and it's a very necessary update to one of its most popular products.

Inside the Surface Laptop 7 is a Qualcomm Snapdragon X Plus or X Elite chipset, and this is a big deal. This is an Arm-based processor with a fully custom design that delivers performance we;ve never seen from WIjdows on Arm in the past. In a lot of cases, it's faster than any current Intel processor, and on top of that, it's far more power efficient. You can get some insane battery life numbers from this laptop, withj our reviewer Rich Woods getting over 15 hours of battery life at one point. You also get up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

 Related
Surface Laptop 7 review: You don't have to wait for Windows on Arm to get good anymore

It does most things right

To go along with the brand-new processor, Microsoft also gave the Surface Laptop 7 a completely new design. Both models are now smaller, and in the case of the 13-inch version, you even get a slightly larger 13.8-inch panel (while still being smaller than its predecessor). This change was long overdue, and to make things better, the screen has also been improved with a brighter panel and better color reproduction.

For ports, the Surface Laptop 7 includes two high-speed USB4 connections, a USB Type-A port, a custom Surface Connect port, and a microSD card reader, plus a headphone jack. That's also an upgrade from previous models, and even without proper Thunderbolt, USB4 gives us capabilities that are almost identical (though external GPUs won't work here).

Best business
Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6

Durable and reliable

9 / 10
$1622 $1699 Save $77

Powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon X Eolite, the Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6 delivers solid performance and excellent battery life in a chassis designed for professionals. It has the same excellent keyboard Lenovo is known for, and even the speakers are quite good.

Pros
  • Qualcomm Snapdragon X Elite chip is fast and super efficient
  • Excellent keyboard and a classic design
  • Powerful speakers
Cons
  • Display in the base model isn't that bright
  • It has a lower tier of the Snapdragon X ELite compared to some other models
$1271 at Lenovo $1393 at Amazon $1622 at CDW

Normally, you might expect to see a Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon in this spot, but the company decided to go with the T14s to debut the new QUalcomm Snapdragon X Elite chip, and frankly,. that alone makes this the laptop to choose if you're looking for a business laptop.

The Snapdragon X Elite chip in the Thinkpad T14s Gen 6 isn't the most high-end configuration available, but make no mistake, this is an excellent chip all the same, and it delivers more performance than its Intel rivals while also being much more efficient, resulting in an experience you can't really get on Intel-based laptops. It lasts over 14 hours on battery, according to our reviewer, who also said the laptop is "perfect for mobile professionals". Aside from the processor, you can also get up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD.

 Related
Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6 (14" Snapdragon) review: A near-perfect business laptop

The Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6 has it all - great performance, long battery life, a comfortable keyboard, and potent speakers in a ultra-light chassis

The model we reviewed came with the base display, which is a FUll HD+ IPS panel, and it could be a bit brighter and vibrant. But if you want that, you can opt for the stunning 2.8K OLED panel, which also comes with a smooth 120Hz refresh rate. If you want the best experience, this is absolutely the way to go, but it's not necessary if you're just using the laptop for work.

The design is as classic ThinkPad as it gets, with the fully black chassis accented by some red highlights, including the iconic TrackPoint and the duplicate mouse buttons above the touchpad. Of course, it has military-grade durability certification, too, so this is a laptop that can last you a long time. And as more developers embrace Windows on Arm, it will probably be getting better over time, too.

Best Windows tablet
Surface Pro 11

Fast, efficient, and super portable

$900 $1000 Save $100

The Surface Pro 11 is the best Windows tablet on the market right now. Powered by a Snapdragon X Elite, it's both fast and efficient with great battery life, and it has a better display than ever, including a new OLED option. It's not the best Windows tablet now, it's the best Surface Pro ever.

Pros
  • Snapdragon X Elite makes it faster than ever while having great battery life
  • It's the best Surface Pro display ever
  • The new Flex Keyboard is a big upgrade
Cons
  • Windows still isn't the best operating system for tablets
$1000 at Best Buy $900 at Amazon

As far as Windows tablets are concerned, no brand is as well-known as the Surface Pro, and for good reason. They've always been some of the best tablets you can buy, and with the Surface Pro 111, that's more true than ever before.

Starting on the inside, the Surface Pro 11 comes with the brand-new Snapdragon X series chips, with options for the Snapdragon X Plus or X Elite. No matter what you choose, you're getting a very powerful machine that can outclass a typical Intel PC in a lot of use cases. But more importantly, it's also much more efficient, so battery life is better than it's ever been on a Surface Pro. As our Editor-in-Chief Rich Woods put it in his review, "it's truly exciting to see a Windows tablet with...tablet battery life".

 Related
Surface Pro 11 review: Microsoft almost gets it right

A great laptop with a bad tablet OS

But it doesn't stop there. This is also the best display Microsoft has ever used on a Surface Pro. Previous displays were good, but not super vibrant and very reflective. Now, Microsoft made it less glossy and you have an OLED option for the first time, which looks great. It's also a very sharp screen with a 120Hz refresh rate, so it's great all around.

If you want to turn the Surface Pro 11 into a proper laptop, Microsoft has also introduced the Flex Keyboard. It's sold separately and pretty expensive, at $400, but it now gives you the ability to use it detached from the tablet, so you have a lot more flexibility. You can use it as a screen cover, but you can also push the screen back if you want to get some distance from your display.

All in all, the Surface Pro 11 is a fantastic device that's easy to recommend to anyone wanting a Windows-powered tablet. We suspect it will remain that way for a long while to come.

Best gaming laptop
Acer Predator Helios 16 (2024)

So much power

9 / 10
$2050 $2300 Save $250

The Acer Predator Helios is a very powerful gaming laptop with top-notch processors and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4080 GPU that can can handle any game without issues. It has lso an excellent display with a 250Hz refresh rate, perfect for the most demanding gamers.

Pros
  • Incredible performance from both the CPU and GPU
  • Excellent display with a super smooth 250Hz refresh rate
  • Lovely design with plenty of RGB
Cons
  • Fans get very loud
  • Battery life is bad
$1900 at Acer $2050 at Micro Center

Gaming laptops need a lot more power than your average laptop, and the Acer Predator Helios 16 is more than happy to deliver on that front. It has all the performance you could want and an excellent display to boot, plus it looks cool.

Starting with performance, we have a laptop with 14th-gen Intel Core HX-series processors, paired with up to an Nvidia geForce RTX 4080 Laptop GPU, which can use the full 175W of power it's designed for. You won't be hungry for power, and any and all games should run easily on this laptop without much sacrifice. In fact, our reviewer called it "one of the most powerful and one of the best laptops of the year", which is very high praise. You do get bad battery life as a result, but that's expected for a gaming laptop.

If that's not enough to convince you, consider this: the Acer Predator comes with a 16-inch QUad HD+ display with a 250Hz refresh rate. That high resolution and refresh rate are frankly more than the laptop's power can deliver in most games, but if you have an esports title you want to play as smoothly as possible, this display delivers. And that's not even mentioning the excellent color reproduction, since the display covers 99% of DCI-P3 based on our measurements.

A powerful laptop like this has to be big and bulky, so portability is a notable sacrifice, but you do get a striking design with a ton of RGB lighting making sure your laptop is going to turn heads if you use it in public.You also get plenty of ports, so there's very little to complain about here.

Best for creators
MacBook Pro (M3, 2023)

So much performance with great battery life

$1699 $1999 Save $300

Despite already having the best creator laptop on the market, Apple updated the MacBook pro for a second time in 2023 with the M3 family of processors, making the CPU and GPU even more powerful and efficient. The latest MacBook Pro is an absolutely fantastic machine for creative workloads, and it can last much longer on battery than any comparable Windows laptop.

Pros
  • Apple M3 Pro and M3 Max processors are ideal for all kinds of content creation
  • Battery life is fantastic thanks to the new 3nm Arm processors
  • Stunning mini-LED display with up to 1600 nits of brightness
  • Six-speaker system makes for an excellent media experience
Cons
  • Some might not like the notch
  • Expensive, especially with storage and RAM upgrades
  • The base M3 14-inch model isn't great for demanding workloads
$1699 at Best Buy (14-inch, M3 Pro/Max) $2399 at Best Buy (16-inch, M3 Pro/Max)

Apple was already way ahead of the pack when it comes to creator laptops, but that didn't stop the company from introducing even more powerful and efficient MacBook Pro models in late 2023 despite the previous models being less than a year old. XDA Senior Editor Ben Sin reviewed the MacBook Pro 16 (M3 Max) and mentioned that those with an M2 model likely won't see a need to upgrade. For everyone else, however, it's the go-to model.

 Related
MacBook Pro 16 (M3 Max) review: Apple’s just flexing

The 10-month old M2 Max MacBook Pro is still more powerful than 99% of laptops out there, then Apple released an upgrade with more graphical prowess

The latest MacBook Pro models feature the M3 family of processors, and creators will want to focus on the M3 Pro and M3 Max versions. These processors are based on a new 3nm process, making them even more efficient than before, delivering unparalleled battery life while offering excellent performance for demanding workloads like video editing and 3D rendering. Of course, they're also faster than ever, particularly the M3 Max model, featuring a faster CPU and a totally new GPU with ray-tracing support. You also get more RAM than ever, with up to 192GB of unified memory, along with up to 8TB of storage.

In terms of the display, the MacBook Pro comes with a 14.2-inch or 16.2-inch mini-LED panel, which are both fantastic. The screen has hundreds of individual dimming zones, delivering high contrast and brightness up to a whopping 1,600 nits, making for a terrific viewing experience. For this generation, the SDR brightness has also been increased to 600 nits, so visibility should be even better outdoors. The resolution varies depending on the size, but both are equally sharp, and to go along with that, you get a six-speaker system, resulting in one of the best media experiences on any laptop. The webcam is also a great 1080p sensor, though there's no facial recognition support. You can use Touch ID, though.

The design of the MacBook Pro is fairly standard, being an all-metal laptop with a clamshell design and starting at 3.5 pounds (for the 14-inch M3 Pro model), which is reasonable considering how powerful it is. The M3 Pro and M3 Max variants also come in a new Space Black colorway, in addition to the classic Silver. As for ports, you get three Thunderbolt 4 connections, along with HDMI 2.1, a headphone jack with support for high-impedance headphones, an SD card reader, and MagSafe 3 for charging.

Dell's new XPS size looks great

8 / 10

Dell's XPS 16 is the new best option for power users and pros who desire extra performance and a larger display. It's taking over for the XPS 17, and it's been redesigned to match the futuristic XPS 13 Plus look that was introduced a couple of years ago.

Pros
  • Beautiful displays with plenty of room to work
  • Lots of performance thanks to Intel Core Ultra CPUs and discrete Nvidia GPUs
  • Overhauled futuristic design
Cons
